rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA A. MCCOLM VS. GOLDEN EAGLE INSURANCE CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Void Order And Voidable Order Re Sanctions Entered August 22, 2007 By Disqualified Commissioner And For Reconsideration Before Judge Who Is Not Disqualified;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 13, 2008 LINE 1. PLAINTIFF PATRICIA MCCOLM'S Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Void Order And Voidable Order Re Sanctions Entered August 22, 2007 By Disqualified Commissioner And For Reconsideration Before Judge Who Is Not Disqualified: MS. MCCOLM'S MOTION TO VACATE ORDER RE SANCTIONS OF AUGUST 22, 2007 IS DENIED. COMMISSIONER CHAN WAS NOT DISQUALIFIED TO ACT. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS, IN EFFECT, A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AND IT IS UNTIMELY. ON THE MERITS, COMMISSIONER CHAN'S ORDER IS SUPPORTED BY THE FACTS AND THE LAW. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA A. MCCOLM VS. GOLDEN EAGLE INSURANCE CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate And Set Aside Void Order And Voidable Order Re Discovery Entered Mar 21, 07 By Disqualified Commissioner And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 13, 2008 LINE 1. PLAINTIFF PATRICIA MCCOLM'S MOTION To Vacate And Set Aside Void Order And Voidable Order Re Discovery Entered March 21, 2007 By Disqualified Commissioner (And Request For Judicial Notice): MS. MCCOLM'S MOTION TO VACATE DISCOVERY ORDER OF MARCH 21, 2007 IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S CHALLENGE TO COMMISSIONER CHAN FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF C.C.P. SECTION 170.4(C)(3) GIVEN THE RULING OF JUDGE INGLE DENYING PLAINTIFF'S C.C.P. SECTION 170.1 CHALLENGE ON OCTOBER 14, 2005. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SET FORTH NEW FACTS AND FACTS ARISING AFTER THE PRIOR CHALLENGE. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: ALL BAY CITIES CONSTRUCTION, A CALIFORNIA VS. NANCY MATTINGLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 13, 2008 LINE 8. CROSS-DEFENDANT ALL BAY CITIES CONSTRUCTION'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT: Cross-defendant's demurrer to the 5th and 8th causes of action is sustained without leave to amend in accordance with Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal. 4th 543. The demurrer to the 9th cause of action is overruled, 10 days to answer. Paragraph 104 alleges that cross-defendant engaged in fraud in procuring a signature on a document and this was done to cause cross-complainant emotional distress. This is a different allegation than the charging allegations in the 5th and 8th causes of action. The demurrer is timely for cross-complainant did not seek a default. There is no legal authority for the award of costs and attorneys' fees in connection with the demurrer. =(302/PJM/AY)
